serving.natural_language_portion: One standard café slice of speculoos cheesecake weighing approximately 120 g, as typically served at a Singapore café.

nutrient_source.title: Cheesecake, prepared from recipe — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB No. 18139) and Cheesecake with biscuit base — USDA Foundation Foods, adapted with speculoos biscuit component adjustment
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Base nutrient values derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for cheesecake prepared from recipe (NDB 18139, ~321 kcal/100g). Values adjusted upward for the speculoos biscuit base and crumb topping contribution, which adds additional carbohydrate, sugar, and fat from caramelised spiced biscuits. Final estimates reflect a composite baked cheesecake with approximately 15–20% speculoos biscuit content by weight. All values are estimates; no Singapore-specific or HPB Foodo entry exists for speculoos cheesecake.

gi_source.title: Cheesecake GI estimation based on analogous baked dairy-egg-sugar dessert; reference: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281–2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for speculoos cheesecake. GI estimated at 46 based on analogous baked cheesecake entries in the University of Sydney GI Database and the 2008 International Tables (Atkinson et al.), which report GI values of 40–55 for baked cheesecakes with biscuit bases. The speculoos biscuit component (caramelised spiced shortcrust) has a GI of approximately 55–60 (analogous to plain sweet biscuits), but its contribution is diluted by the high-fat, high-protein cream cheese matrix, which suppresses the overall GI. Composite GI estimated at 46 (low category). GL per 120 g serving = (46 × 32.0 × 1.20) / 100 = 17.7, rounded to 17.8.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~210 mg/100g) and saturated fat
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to half a standard slice, as a 120 g serving contributes approximately 252 mg sodium (~11% of the 2300 mg daily limit) and significant saturated fat, both of which can adversely affect blood pressure and vascular tone.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content (~22 g/100g) and moderate glycemic load per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should treat speculoos cheesecake as an occasional indulgence only; a standard 120 g slice delivers approximately 26 g of sugar and a glycemic load of ~18, which can cause meaningful postprandial glucose elevation and should be accounted for in the daily carbohydrate budget.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~11.8 g/100g) and cholesterol (~95 mg/100g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Speculoos cheesecake is high in saturated fat and dietary cholesterol from cream cheese, eggs, and butter; individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should limit consumption to small occasional portions and prioritise lower-saturated-fat dessert alternatives in their regular diet.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Speculoos cheesecake is a baked Western-style dessert that combines a classic cream cheese filling with the distinctive caramelised, cinnamon-spiced flavour of speculoos biscuits (most commonly Lotus Biscoff). The base is formed from crushed speculoos biscuits bound with melted butter, providing a crunchy, aromatic foundation. The filling is a smooth, dense mixture of cream cheese, sugar, eggs, and cream, baked until just set. The top is finished with additional speculoos crumbs or a drizzle of cookie butter spread, adding textural contrast and intensifying the warm spice notes. The flavour profile is rich, sweet, and warmly spiced with cinnamon, nutmeg, and brown sugar caramel undertones. In Singapore, this dessert is commonly served at Western-style cafés and dessert bars as a premium slice cake, priced around SGD 9–12 per slice. Nutritionally, it is energy-dense (~330 kcal/100g), high in saturated fat (~11.8 g/100g), and high in sugar (~22 g/100g), making it a treat food rather than an everyday item. The glycemic index is estimated at 46 (low category), but the glycemic load per standard serving is moderate (~18), which is clinically relevant for individuals managing blood glucose. The dish is not suitable for vegans, is not gluten-free, and may contain soy traces from the biscuit component. It is relevant to clinical dietary counselling for Type 2 Diabetes, cardiovascular disease, and hypertension due to its saturated fat, sugar, and sodium content.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for speculoos cheesecake exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or any other approved primary source. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy cheesecake (NDB 18139) with compositional adjustment for the speculoos biscuit component. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008. Medium confidence is appropriate given the adaptation basis and absence of a direct match.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy cheesecake (NDB 18139) adapted for speculoos biscuit base and topping (~15–20% biscuit content by weight). No HPB Foodo or Singapore-specific entry exists for this dish. 2. GI estimated at 46 based on analogous baked cheesecake entries in the University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care); the high fat and protein content of the cream cheese matrix suppresses the overall GI relative to the biscuit component alone. 3. Halal status is listed as false because speculoos biscuits and cream cheese sourcing at the specific restaurant (The Tree Cafe – Funan) has not been verified against MUIS certification; consumers should verify with the restaurant directly. 4. All -1 values in per_100g represent nutrients not reported in the adapted USDA source for this food category. 5. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are estimated from USDA SR Legacy cheesecake data and are not independently verified for this specific speculoos variant. 6. Multilingual names for Chinese, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi are provided per Singaporean dish requirements, as this item is served in a Singapore café context. 7. The food is served at The Tree Cafe – Funan (GrabFood Singapore, 1 restaurant). Price: SGD 9.50.
